description: A world beneath the surface where sunlight is a memory and the stone presses close with the weight of the earth above.
biomes: Caves
Rough stone chambers where water drips in the dark and your torch light barely reaches the ceiling.
Tunnels
Narrow passages bored through solid rock, some natural, some carved by things that prefer the dark.
Flooded
Passages half-filled with black water that reflects your light back at you. The way forward means getting wet.
Mines
Old shafts braced with rotting timber. Pick marks line the walls and abandoned carts sit rusting on iron rails.
Caverns
Vast underground halls where stalactites hang like cathedral pillars and your voice echoes for longer than feels right.
Underdark
A sprawling network of lightless tunnels where entire civilizations thrive in the absence of the sun. Nothing here is friendly.
Deep
So far below the surface that the stone is warm to the touch. The pressure is immense and the silence total.
Lava Tubes
Smooth-walled tunnels carved by rivers of molten rock long cooled. The floor is glassy and the air still carries faint warmth.
Crystal Caves
Walls studded with formations that catch even the faintest light and scatter it into prismatic shards across the dark.
Mushroom Caverns
Pale fungi the size of trees growing in the damp dark. A faint glow comes from the caps, and the air tastes earthy.
Abandoned Dwellings
Empty rooms carved from the rock, still furnished with the remains of lives lived in the deep. Dust covers everything.
